issue.author: dtcxzyw

comments.author: fhahn
comments.body: Hmm, cannot reproduce with plain opt.

comments.author: fhahn
comments.body: Ok reproduced with custom use list order. Will prepare a fix soon

comments.author: antoniofrighetto
comments.body: Just out of curiosity, when querying AA, we are expecting `canInstructionRangeModRef` to return true on the first load popped out (`%retval.sroa.25.0.copyload`) here, correct?

comments.author: fhahn
comments.body:
> Just out of curiosity, when querying AA, we are expecting `canInstructionRangeModRef` to return true on the first load popped out (`%retval.sroa.25.0.copyload`) here, correct?

No, AFAICT AA is working correctly, but the results are used for incorrect caching, see https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/84835

comments.author: antoniofrighetto
comments.body:
> > Just out of curiosity, when querying AA, we are expecting `canInstructionRangeModRef` to return true on the first load popped out (`%retval.sroa.25.0.copyload`) here, correct?
> 
> No, AFAICT AA is working correctly, but the results are used for incorrect caching, see #84835

Makes sense, thanks (didn't see the PR opened by the time had commented).

comments.author: fhahn
comments.body:
> Makes sense, thanks (didn't see the PR opened by the time had commented).

Unfortunately Github does not seem to notify when a PR mentions an issue :(

issue.title: [ArgPromotion] Miscompile at -O3

tests.test_body:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"

@f = external dso_local global { i16, i64 }, align 8

define hidden void @callee1(ptr nocapture noundef readonly %q, i1 %c) {
entry:
  br i1 %c, label %then, label %exit

then:                                             ; preds = %entry
  store i16 123, ptr @f, align 8
  br label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %then, %entry
  %l.0 = load i16, ptr %q, align 8
  %gep.8 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %q, i64 8
  %l.1 = load i64, ptr %gep.8, align 8
  call void @use(i16 %l.0, i64 %l.1)
  ret void
}

declare void @use(i16, i64)
